engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Siena Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Siena in the UK as grey imports, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All prices reflect supply-only ranges from vetted UK specialists.

cards.altText: Fiat Siena 188A4.000 engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine causes valve damage if belt snaps, replace every 60k miles.
Injector seal leaks - fuel smell and misfires common on high-mileage grey import units.
Cooling system wear - radiator and water pump failures on high-mileage imports under UK load.

cards.altText: Fiat Siena 199A2.000 engine - diesel 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - loss of power and rough idle common on high-mileage import diesels.
Turbo wastegate actuator failure - boost pressure loss triggers P2262 codes on grey imports.
DPF regeneration issues - soot load warnings on UK short-journey driving patterns.

cards.altText: Fiat Siena 350A1.000 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Camshaft position sensor failure - intermittent misfire codes on older grey import units.
PCV valve wear - increased oil consumption on high-mileage Fire petrol engines.
Exhaust manifold cracking - flange leaks at catalytic converter on import-spec engines.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,000-£1,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Fire engine units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Siena Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 4 1.3L MultiJet introduced 2006, common-rail injection with EGR - dominant grey import diesel.
compatibleVariants: EX 1.3 MultiJet, Project 323 1.3 Diesel, HLX 1.3 MultiJet
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - loss of power on high-mileage imports, 60k+ miles typical
Turbo wastegate actuator failure - P2262 codes, boost pressure loss
DPF regeneration issues - soot load warnings on UK short-journey patterns

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 5 update to MultiJet with DPF compatibility, improved emissions for later grey imports.
compatibleVariants: Grand Siena 1.3 MultiJet, Project 323 LCI 1.3 Diesel, EX Euro 5
commonFailures: DPF filter blockage - ash accumulation requires cleaning or replacement
AdBlue system prep needed for ULEZ compliance on some UK-registered imports
EGR cooler clogging persists from earlier MultiJet design
entries.cta: Get quotes for A13DTE Fiat Siena engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel

compatibleVariants: EX 1.9 JTD, Project 178 1.9 Diesel, HLX 1.9 JTD
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks - fuel smell and rough idle on high-mileage units
Turbo wear - boost loss and blue smoke on older import examples
Timing belt tensioner failure - critical service item on interference engine

commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine causes catastrophic valve damage, replace every 60k miles
Injector seal leaks - fuel smell and misfires on high-mileage imports

commonFailures: Timing belt failure - same interference risk as 188A4.000, belt kit essential

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Siena Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Siena's grey import engines face specific reliability challenges in UK conditions. The 1.2L Fire petrol and 1.3L MultiJet diesel dominate replacement enquiries, with timing belt failures and EGR clogging the most common causes. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Fiat Siena 1.2 Fire Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Siena 1.2 Fire Timing Belt Failure - Why Interference Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Siena ELX, HLX, SX 1.2 Fire (1996-2013, codes 188A4.000 & 188A5.000)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt service intervals missed
problems.rootCause: The Fire petrol is an interference engine - if the timing belt snaps, pistons strike open valves causing catastrophic internal damage. Grey import vehicles often have incomplete service histories.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing belt, tensioner, idler pulley replacement with water pump service
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure if done before belt degradation; essential for grey imports

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve replacement, head gasket, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block is undamaged; often not cost-effective on £300-£1,500 Siena

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt Fire engine with new timing kit, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for grey imports; includes upgraded components addressing original failure

problems.recommendation: On a Fiat Siena worth £300-£1,500, preventative belt replacement is the only cost-effective option before failure occurs. Once valve damage happens, a rebuilt engine with included timing kit offers better long-term value than intermediate repairs on a budget import.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 188A4.000 Fire engine prices for your Fiat Siena

problems.h4: Fiat Siena 1.3 MultiJet EGR Clogging - Why Import Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Siena EX, Project 323 1.3 MultiJet (2006-2017, codes 199A2.000 & A13DTE)
problems.rootCause: EGR valves recirculate exhaust gases to reduce emissions; on import diesels from high-mileage markets, carbon buildup clogs the valve and cooler, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve removal, ultrasonic cleaning, or blanking plate fitment
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may trigger MoT emissions concerns on Euro 5+ imports

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or reconditioned EGR valve and cooler assembly with coding
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptom but doesn't prevent recurrence on high-mileage import engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt MultiJet with upgraded EGR components, DPF service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for grey imports; includes EGR update and UK fuel compatibility checks

problems.recommendation: For grey import Sienas with 80k+ miles, EGR cleaning is a short-term fix. A rebuilt MultiJet with upgraded EGR components offers better reliability and includes UK fuel adaptation - critical for import vehicles running on UK diesel.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 199A2.000 MultiJet engine replacement UK

problems.h4: Fiat Siena Fuel Compatibility Issues - Why Import Engines Struggle on UK Petrol
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Siena grey imports, especially Brazilian FlexFuel and European-market units (1996-2017)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Variable - issues often appear after first UK fill-up or seasonal temperature changes
problems.rootCause: Brazilian FlexFuel engines and some European imports have fuel system calibrations optimised for ethanol blends or different octane ratings; running on standard UK unleaded can cause misfires, poor economy, and long-term injector wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: ECU remap for UK petrol, injector seal replacement, fuel filter change
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses compatibility; essential for FlexFuel imports converted to UK petrol use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector ultrasonic cleaning, flow testing, coding to ECU for UK fuel specs
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance but doesn't prevent future compatibility issues on import engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt Fire engine with UK fuel calibration, new injector seals, MoT emissions prep
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; ensures long-term compatibility with UK petrol for grey imports

problems.recommendation: If your Siena is a Brazilian FlexFuel import, fuel adaptation is essential before any engine work. For European imports showing fuel-related symptoms, a rebuilt engine with UK calibration offers peace of mind and prevents repeat issues.
problems.cta: Verify UK fuel compatibility for your Fiat Siena engine code

problems.h4: Fiat Siena Turbocharger Failure - Why MultiJet Imports Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Siena 1.3 MultiJet, 1.9 JTD diesel variants (2006-2017, codes 199A2.000, A13DTE, 182B9.000)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000-130,000 miles on high-mileage grey import diesels
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuators on import MultiJet engines suffer from carbon buildup and mechanical wear; UK stop-start driving and short journeys accelerate actuator failure, triggering P2262 boost codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Wastegate actuator replacement or repair with boost calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate boost loss; may not prevent recurrence on high-mileage import turbos

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo removal, bearing replacement, balancing, actuator calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance but doesn't address underlying wear on import-spec turbos

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with revised turbo actuator, UK calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; includes turbo update and UK driving condition adaptation

problems.recommendation: For grey import Sienas over 100k miles, actuator repair is a temporary fix. A rebuilt MultiJet with revised turbo components offers better reliability under UK driving patterns and includes comprehensive warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare MultiJet turbo replacement costs for your Fiat Siena

problems.h4: Fiat Siena Immobiliser Start Issues - Why Engine Swaps Fail on Grey Imports
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Siena grey imports with ECU-coded engines (1996-2017, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: N/A - issue occurs post-replacement if ECU/VIN mismatch not addressed
problems.rootCause: Grey import engine swaps often trigger immobiliser lockout because replacement ECUs aren't pre-coded to the vehicle's VIN; this prevents starting even with a mechanically sound engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Immobiliser reset, VIN recoding, key reprogramming by specialist
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves start issue but requires specialist equipment; not all garages can perform

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement ECU with basic coding, immobiliser match to existing keys
repairOptions.longevity: Works but may lack full UK emissions calibration for grey import engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with ECU pre-coded to VIN, immobiliser matched, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; prevents start issues and ensures UK compliance for grey imports

problems.recommendation: Never fit a grey import engine without confirming ECU pre-coding. EngineMarket's network supplies rebuilt units with immobiliser coding included - preventing costly start failures and ensuring your Siena returns to the road quickly.
problems.cta: Get quotes for pre-coded Fiat Siena engine replacement UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Siena
finalCta.paragraph: From timing belt catastrophes to EGR clogging and fuel compatibility concerns, Fiat Siena grey imports face specific challenges on UK roads. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand import vehicle requirements. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and supply & fit options, get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Siena Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Siena and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Fiat Siena Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Siena as a grey import is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help identify your exact replacement requirement.

items.title: Fiat Siena 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3L MultiJet diesel powered most grey import Siena diesel variants from 2006 onwards, offering strong fuel economy and torque for UK driving - but EGR clogging and DPF issues on high-mileage imports drive significant replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging causing power loss
turbo wastegate actuator failure triggering P2262 codes
DPF regeneration issues on UK short-journey patterns
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Siena 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Siena 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The older 1.9L JTD diesel powered early grey import Siena models before the MultiJet introduction - a robust but aging unit where parts availability must be verified before supply for Project 178 era vehicles.
commonFailurePoints: Injector seal leaks causing fuel smell
timing belt tensioner failure on interference engine
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Siena 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Siena 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L Fire petrol is the most common engine fitted to grey import Fiat Siena models - a simple SOHC 8-valve design that's affordable to rebuild but requires strict timing belt maintenance due to its interference configuration.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causing catastrophic valve damage on interference engine
injector seal leaks leading to fuel smell
cooling system wear on high-mileage imports
items.cta: Compare Fiat Siena 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Siena 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L Fire petrol offered increased capacity for higher-spec grey import Siena variants - same reliable SOHC design as the 1.2L but with more power, though exhaust manifold cracking and sensor failures become more common on older units.
commonFailurePoints: Exhaust manifold cracking at catalytic converter flange
camshaft position sensor failure causing intermittent misfires
oil pump wear reducing lubrication pressure on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Siena 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Siena 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The rare 1.6L 16-valve Fire petrol powered high-spec grey import Siena variants - offering performance-oriented output but with higher replacement costs and limited availability due to low UK import volumes of this configuration.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure on interference engine causing valve damage
dual mass flywheel wear leading to transmission shudder
cylinder head gasket failure on overheated high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Siena 1.6L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Fiat Siena Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Siena's grey import production timeline, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt engine prices from specialists.

years.description: The original Project 178 Siena launched with simple Fire petrol and JTD diesel engines - robust designs that remain common in the UK grey import market today.
keyChanges: Fiat Siena Project 178 platform introduced for South American and European markets
1.2L Fire petrol (188A4.000) and 1.9L JTD diesel (182B9.000) become dominant grey import engines
Early units lack Euro 4 emissions equipment, simplifying UK compliance for imports
knownFor: Simple SOHC Fire petrol design affordable to rebuild for budget-conscious import owners
Timing belt critical on interference engines - missed service causes catastrophic valve damage
1.9L JTD robust but injector seal leaks common on high-mileage grey import examples
Parts interchange with Fiat Palio/Strada simplifies sourcing for Project 178 engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2006 Fiat Siena replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Siena 1.2 Fire timing belt replacement • Fiat Siena 1.9 JTD injector seal leak fix • Project 178 engine code verification UK
enquiries: Fiat Siena 1.2 Fire timing belt replacement
Fiat Siena 1.9 JTD injector seal leak fix

years.description: The facelifted Project 323 introduced the 1.3L MultiJet diesel and revised Euro 4 Fire petrol codes - improving emissions but adding complexity that drives replacement enquiries on grey imports.
keyChanges: 1.3L MultiJet diesel (199A2.000) replaces older 1.9L JTD for most grey import diesel variants
Euro 4 Fire petrol codes (188A5.000, 350A1.000) introduced with updated emissions components
EGR systems become standard on diesel variants, creating new failure patterns for UK imports
mainEngines: 188A5.000 - 1.2L petrol Euro 4 (revised Fire code for Project 323)
199A2.000 - 1.3L MultiJet diesel Euro 4 (new dominant import diesel)
knownFor: MultiJet EGR valve clogging becomes dominant failure pattern on high-mileage grey import diesels
Revised Fire codes improve emissions but timing belt remains critical on interference engines
Fuel compatibility verification essential for European-market imports running on UK fuels
Project 323 engines most commonly searched for replacement in UK grey import market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2013 Fiat Siena replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Siena 1.3 MultiJet EGR clogging fix • Project 323 engine code 188A5.000 verification • Fiat Siena Euro 4 emissions compliance UK
enquiries: Fiat Siena 1.3 MultiJet EGR clogging fix
Fiat Siena Euro 4 emissions compliance UK

years.preview: Grand Siena: Brazilian Market Imports & FlexFuel Adaptation
years.description: The Grand Siena introduced Brazilian-market specifications including FlexFuel capability - requiring specialist adaptation for UK petrol use and creating new compatibility considerations for grey import replacements.
keyChanges: Grand Siena platform launched for Brazilian market, later imported to UK as grey imports
FlexFuel engine variants (350A1.000 adapted) require ECU recalibration for UK unleaded petrol
Euro 5 MultiJet diesel (A13DTE) with DPF introduced, adding AdBlue system considerations for ULEZ
mainEngines: 350A1.000 - 1.4L Fire petrol (Grand Siena base, FlexFuel adaptation available)
A13DTE - 1.3L MultiJet diesel Euro 5 with DPF (Grand Siena diesel variants)
popularModels: Fiat Grand Siena FlexFuel (adapted for UK)
knownFor: FlexFuel adaptation essential for Brazilian imports running on standard UK unleaded petrol
DPF regeneration issues on Grand Siena MultiJet diesels under UK short-journey driving patterns
Lower UK import volumes mean longer lead times for Grand Siena replacement engines
Euro 5 emissions equipment requires specialist calibration for UK MoT compliance

faq.h2: Fiat Siena Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Siena engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Fiat Siena engine replacement costs range from £350-£800 for used units to £950-£1,700 for rebuilt engines, depending on engine code and variant. Grey import status affects pricing due to parts availability and compatibility verification requirements.
keyPoints: Used engines: £350-£800 with mileage verification reports included
Rebuilt Fire petrol: £950-£1,400 with timing belt kit essential for interference protection
Rebuilt MultiJet diesel: £1,100-£1,700 with EGR update for import reliability
Supply & fit service adds £400-£750 to engine price depending on location
items.cta: Compare Fiat Siena engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine code does my grey import Fiat Siena have?
items.answer: Check your engine plate or VIN decoder for codes like 188A4.000 (1.2L Fire petrol) or 199A2.000 (1.3L MultiJet diesel) - critical for ordering compatible replacement engines for grey import Siena vehicles.
keyPoints: Engine plate located on cylinder head or timing cover shows factory code
VIN decoder tools confirm engine code against chassis number for import verification
Common codes: 188A4.000/188A5.000 (1.2L Fire), 199A2.000/A13DTE (1.3L MultiJet)
Code verification essential before ordering to avoid compatibility issues on grey imports
items.cta: Verify your Fiat Siena engine code before ordering replacement

items.question: Is the Fiat Siena 1.2 Fire engine reliable as a grey import?
items.answer: The 1.2L Fire petrol is generally reliable on grey import Siena models if timing belt maintenance is strictly followed - rebuilt units with included timing kits offer best long-term value for budget import owners.
keyPoints: Fire petrol simple SOHC design affordable to rebuild for cost-conscious import owners
Timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage - replace every 60k miles without exception
Injector seal leaks common on high-mileage imports but easily addressed during rebuild
Rebuilt Fire engines with timing kit included offer best reliability for grey import Siena
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Fiat Siena 1.2 Fire engine with timing kit

items.question: Can I fit a grey import Fiat Siena engine myself in the UK?
items.answer: Supply-only engines available for DIY installation, but specialist fitting recommended for grey imports due to ECU immobiliser coding requirements and import-specific compatibility considerations.
keyPoints: Supply-only option available for experienced DIY mechanics with proper tools
ECU immobiliser coding essential post-installation to prevent start failures on imports
Specialist garages experienced with grey imports ensure correct fuel calibration and emissions compliance
Warranty validity may require professional installation at approved UK garage
items.cta: Find Fiat Siena engine supply & fit specialists for grey import vehicles

items.question: What warranty comes with a grey import Fiat Siena replacement engine?
items.answer: 6-12 month warranty standard on rebuilt grey import Siena engines, covering internal components at UK approved garages - essential protection for budget import vehicles with limited residual value.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt engines from EngineMarket network
Warranty valid at any UK approved garage, not just original supplier location
Covers internal components: bearings, pistons, valves, gaskets against manufacturing defects
Used engines may carry shorter 3-6 month warranty depending on mileage and verification status
items.cta: Compare Fiat Siena engine warranty terms from UK import specialists

items.question: How long does grey import Fiat Siena engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Typical timeline: 5-10 days for engine delivery plus 1-2 days fitting equals 7-14 days total from order to road-ready for most grey import Siena engine replacements.
keyPoints: Next-day dispatch available on common codes like 188A4.000 and 199A2.000 if ordered by 2pm
Rare codes like 182B6.000 may require 3-5 day sourcing lead time from specialist suppliers
Fitting takes 1-2 days depending on garage workload and whether ECU coding required
Nationwide delivery with tracking and insurance included for grey import engine shipments
items.cta: Check Fiat Siena engine delivery times for your postcode

items.question: Will a grey import replacement Fiat Siena engine pass UK MoT emissions?
items.answer: All replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket are pre-tested to pass UK emissions standards for the relevant grey import model year, with documentation provided for MoT certification.
keyPoints: Engines pre-tested on dyno to verify emissions compliance before dispatch to UK customers
Documentation provided confirms engine meets emissions standards for model year and fuel type
Euro 3/4/5 engines matched to vehicle registration year for MoT compliance on grey imports
Specialist suppliers ensure correct calibration for UK fuel specifications where adaptation required
items.cta: Get MoT-compliant Fiat Siena replacement engine quotes UK

items.question: Is a grey import Fiat Siena engine ULEZ compliant in London?
items.answer: Most grey import Fiat Siena petrol engines (1.2L/1.4L Fire) meet Euro 3/4 standards qualifying for ULEZ exemption; diesel variants require Euro 6 for exemption - verify your engine code before ordering.
keyPoints: Petrol Fire engines typically Euro 3/4 compliant - check engine code for ULEZ eligibility
MultiJet diesel engines usually Euro 4/5 - may incur ULEZ charges in London without retrofit
AdBlue system prep available on A13DTE codes for potential ULEZ compliance upgrades
Enter registration on EngineMarket tool to confirm your Siena's ULEZ status before ordering
items.cta: Verify ULEZ compliance for your Fiat Siena engine code

items.question: Can I adapt a Brazilian FlexFuel Fiat Siena engine for UK petrol?
items.answer: Yes - specialist FlexFuel adaptation service available for Brazilian import Siena engines converted to run on standard UK unleaded petrol, recommended before ordering replacement units.
keyPoints: FlexFuel engines detect ethanol/petrol blends automatically - requires ECU recalibration for UK unleaded
Adaptation includes injector seal replacement and fuel system compatibility verification
Specialist conversion recommended to prevent long-term fuel system damage on UK petrol
Confirm adaptation service included when ordering replacement for Brazilian market grey imports
items.cta: Enquire about FlexFuel to UK petrol conversion for Fiat Siena engines

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt, remanufactured and used grey import Fiat Siena engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt offers best value with critical component updates; remanufactured provides factory-spec restoration for higher-spec imports; used is budget option with verification - choose based on your Siena's value and intended ownership period.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: Critical wear parts replaced, timing kit/EGR update included, 12-month warranty - best value
Remanufactured: All new internal components, factory tolerances restored, 24-month warranty available
Used: Verified low-mileage import units, budget option £350-£800, shorter warranty period
All options available through EngineMarket network with import compatibility verification
